summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across the northern front, while Russian forces claimed liberation of a village in Zaporozhye. The United States advanced a new aid package for Ukraine and Finland prepared to intercept Ukrainian drones over its airspace after a strike on St. Petersburg. Russia increased subsidies to its oil refineries amid intensified drone attacks.
key_points: Ukrainian UAVs were observed approaching Kharkiv from the northwest (Source: @kpszsu/64034).
A UAV moved along the Belarus border in northern Kyiv region heading west (Source: @kpszsu/64046).
Russian Ministry of Defense announced liberation of Gulyaipolskoye village in Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30678).
Finland said it was ready to shoot down Ukrainian drones if they entered Finnish airspace after a strike on St. Petersburg (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30666).
The US House supported a bill providing $8 billion in military credits and additional sanctions against Russia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30664).
Poland imposed flight restrictions near its borders with Belarus and Ukraine from 10 June to 10 September (Source: @UkraineNow/81670).
Russia paid 204.3 billion rubles to its oil refineries in May, a five‑fold increase year‑on‑year, citing drone attacks on the facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81662).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV activity intensified across the northern front, with flights toward Kharkiv, Kyiv and Sumy regions. Russia announced the recapture of Gulyaipolskoye village in Zaporozhye and increased subsidies to oil refineries amid drone attacks. The United States moved forward on a major aid package while Finland prepared to intercept Ukrainian drones over its airspace.

points: The US House of Representatives backed a bill allocating $8 billion in military credits, extending the Ukraine Security Assistance Initiative to 2027, and imposing tougher sanctions on Russia's oil sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30664).
Zelensky published an open letter to Putin proposing a ceasefire, prisoner exchange and negotiations, citing over 30 000 Russian casualties (Source: @DeepStateUA/23550).
Three civilians were killed and a driver and passenger were killed in a car attack on the highway between Novonikolayevka and Trudovoe in the Melitopol district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Ukrainian forces reported advancing up to 800 meters in eleven sectors on the Sumy direction, with ongoing infantry battles in Ivolzhansk and Pisarevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
A Ukrainian ambulance was deliberately hit in Kherson, wounding three medical workers and causing at least two civilian deaths in nearby Yampil, Sumy (Source: @UkraineNow/81677).

Russia paid 204.3 billion rubles ($2.8 billion) to its oil refineries in May, a five‑fold increase from the previous year, to offset price differentials and compensate for drone attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81662).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of the village Gulyaipolskoye (Komsomolskoye) in the Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30678).
A warning was issued about a high threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67489).
President Putin thanked Mirziyoyev for an invitation to St. Petersburg and highlighted Russia's cooperation with Uzbekistan on a nuclear power project (Source: @tass_agency/379190).
Russian officials claimed that European nations ignored the reburial of Nazi victims in Ukraine (Source: @tass_agency/379171).
Ukrainian forces attacked Crimea and Sevastopol overnight, killing three and injuring seven in Simferopol, and shot down more than 20 drones over Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
A massive UAV attack hit the Melitopol urban district, damaging a meat‑processing plant and injuring one worker (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
In the Rostov region, UAVs were destroyed in Millerovo, Chertkovo and Sholokhovo districts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
A civilian car was struck on the highway between Novonikolayevka and Trudovoe in the Melitopol municipal district, killing driver and passenger (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
A general‑education school in Melitopol was attacked, injuring six civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
A UAV was reported heading toward Kharkiv from the northwest (Source: @kpszsu/64034).
A UAV moved along the Belarus border in northern Kyiv region, heading west (Source: @kpszsu/64046).
UAV activity was noted north of Sumy, moving south‑westward (Source: @kpszsu/64012).
UAVs were observed east of Dnipropetrovsk near Vasylkivka, traveling westward (Source: @kpszsu/64040).
Multiple Shahed drones were tracked in the Chernihiv‑Poltava border area moving toward Kyiv and Boryspil districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67485).
Russian forces hit an ambulance in Kherson, wounding three medical staff; separate attacks in Yampil, Sumy caused at least two civilian deaths (Source: @UkraineNow/81677).
Ukrainian UAV attacks targeted a gas station in Rylysk, Kursk region, injuring a man (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
In Belgorod's Grayvoronsky district, an FPV drone strike killed a man in Kozinka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
A drone hit a KAMAZ truck in Malomikhailovka, Belgorod, injuring a driver (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Ukrainian forces reported infantry battles on the Sumy direction, advancing up to 800 meters in several sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
More than 20 drones were shot down over Sevastopol during the night operation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Ukrainian Shahed drones were tracked in multiple northern sectors, including Chernihiv, Poltava and Kyiv regions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67485).
Finland announced readiness to intercept Ukrainian drones over its airspace after a strike on St. Petersburg (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30666).
Poland imposed temporary flight restrictions near its borders with Belarus and Ukraine, affecting low‑altitude flights (Source: @UkraineNow/81670).
A high threat level was issued for Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67489).

key_developments: Ukrainian UAVs approached Kharkiv from the northwest, indicating increased aerial pressure on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/64034).
Russia announced the liberation of Gulyaipolskoye village in Zaporozhye, suggesting a localized territorial gain (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30678).
Finland prepared to shoot down Ukrainian drones over its airspace after a Ukrainian strike on St. Petersburg (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30666).
The US House supported a bill providing $8 billion in military credits and expanded sanctions against Russia (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30664).
Russia paid 204.3 billion rubles to oil refineries in May, a five‑fold increase, to offset damages from Ukrainian drone attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81662).
Poland instituted flight restrictions along its borders with Belarus and Ukraine from 10 June to 10 September (Source: @UkraineNow/81670).
A high threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches was reported from Bryansk Oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67489).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV activity is intensifying across the northern front, threatening logistics and civilian infrastructure in Kharkiv, Kyiv and Sumy regions. Russia's increased financial support to oil refineries underscores its vulnerability to drone strikes, while the potential deployment of Iskander‑M missiles from Bryansk raises the risk of high‑precision strikes on Ukrainian positions. Western aid momentum, exemplified by the US bill, may bolster Ukrainian defensive capabilities but also risks escalating retaliatory actions from Russia.
sections.regional_response: Finland's readiness to intercept Ukrainian drones reflects heightened regional vigilance following cross‑border strikes. Poland's temporary flight restrictions aim to safeguard its airspace amid increased UAV activity near its borders. NATO officials reiterated commitment to prevent Russian victory, indicating continued diplomatic and material support for Ukraine.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths in the northern sectors should be prioritized to anticipate potential incursions into civilian areas. The financial surge to Russian refineries suggests a strategic effort to sustain fuel production despite drone damage, warranting surveillance of refinery sites. The Iskander‑M threat from Bryansk requires early warning systems to mitigate possible missile launches.
